description: A module built for one runtime is loaded in another, so it declares imports the host cannot supply and fails to instantiate.
body: Failure mode: a module compiled for wasm32-wasi (expecting WASI syscall imports) is loaded in a browser, or a browser-targeted module is run in a bare WASI host, so instantiation throws because the import object cannot satisfy the module's declared imports. Detection signals: instantiation fails with a missing-import or unknown-import error; the toolchain target does not match where the module runs; WASI imports requested in a browser. Do not assume a module is portable across runtimes. Safer fallback: pick the target for the actual runtime (wasm32-unknown-unknown + wasm-bindgen for browsers, wasm32-wasi for WASI hosts), provide or shim the needed imports, and verify the module instantiates with every import satisfied.
